metadata.summary: PMP exam ECO changes effective July 1, 2026. People 42%→33%, Process 50%→41%, Business Environment 8%→26%.

content.title: PM Certification Exam Changes 2026
content.summary: The PMP exam ECO (Examination Content Outline) changes effective July 1, 2026: People domain drops from 42% to 33%, Process drops from 50% to 41%, and Business Environment rises from 8% to 26%. The +18% shift requires new emphasis on business case development, benefits realization, strategic alignment, governance and compliance, sustainability, and tailoring.

why.p: Exam prep materials built for the 2021 ECO weight Business Environment at 8% — well below the new 26%. Candidates who study old outlines, training courses, or flashcard decks without updating them will be under-prepared for the largest weight area on the new exam. Materials must be rebuilt from the 2026 ECO PDF, not paraphrased from memory.

list.item: Preparing PMP/CAPM candidates for the July 1, 2026 exam revision.
Updating internal PM training curricula or study-guide repos against the new domain weights.
Building a study-companion agent that needs a current domain weight map.
Auditing existing exam prep materials (Rita Mulcahy, PM PrepCast, internal decks) to find content gaps.
Candidates sitting before the July 1, 2026 cutover — the old outline is still authoritative.
Non-PMI certifications (PRINCE2, IPMA, AgilePM, Scrum.org) — different bodies, different outlines.
General PM coaching that is not exam-bound — exam weights distort real-world emphasis.
Practitioners who already passed — this content is exam-shaped, not job-shaped.
Business Environment: 8% → 26% (+18%)

rule.statement: Rebuild curriculum and study plans from the official 2026 ECO PDF, not from memory or legacy Rita Mulcahy / PM PrepCast outlines; domain weight drift is the most common gap in auto-generated study plans.
rule.rationale: LLMs and trainers fed old outlines (42/50/8) will silently produce materials weighted for the 2021 exam. A candidate spending 8% effort on Business Environment in 2026 is under-prepared for 26% of the exam.

list.item: Business case development and benefits realization
Strategic alignment of projects to organizational objectives
Governance and compliance (regulatory, ethical, organizational)
Sustainability considerations in project delivery
Value delivery measurement and reporting
Tailoring approaches to fit project context

rule.statement: Track candidate weak areas at the ECO enabler level, not the domain level; domain-level coaching is too coarse to direct effective study.
rule.rationale: A candidate scoring 60% on Business Environment might score 90% on strategy and 30% on compliance. Domain-level visibility hides the real gap.

section.name: Agentic Workflow
section.p: A cert-curriculum-curator ingests the official 2026 ECO PDF plus PMBOK 7e / Agile Practice Guide / Process Groups Practice Guide, builds a topic graph (domain → task → enabler → study resources). A gap-analyzer diffs current course materials against the graph and outputs a missing-topic list. A mock-exam-author generates situational questions weighted to the new domain split, with rationales that cite source pages. A weak-area-coach reads candidate quiz history, identifies low-confidence enablers, and queues targeted drills. Human PMP-certified instructor reviews every mock question before it ships — never publish auto-generated PMP items unreviewed.

list.item: cert-curriculum-curator (sonnet) — owns the ECO graph, reconciles drift versus PMI announcements.
gap-analyzer (sonnet) — diffs internal materials against the 2026 ECO; emits missing-topic backlog.
mock-exam-author (sonnet) — drafts situational questions, four answer choices, distractor design, source citation.
weak-area-coach (haiku) — chats with candidate, picks next 5 questions from the bank by skill gap.
policy-watch-agent (haiku, scheduled) — polls PMI announcements + PM Network blog for ECO addenda.

section.name: Prompt Pattern — Mock Exam Author
section.code: You are a mock-exam-author. Generate ONE situational question for ECO domain "Business Environment", task "T1 Plan and Manage Project Compliance", enabler "identify regulatory requirements". Return STRICT JSON: { "stem": "...", "options": ["A","B","C","D"], "correct": "B", "rationale": "...", "source": "PMBOK 7e p.NN", "difficulty": "medium" } Rules: scenario must be 4–6 sentences, distractors must be plausible PMI language, no trick wording, no time-bound details that go stale.

section.name: Prompt Pattern — Gap Analyzer
section.code: You are a gap-analyzer. Inputs: 2026 ECO graph (JSON) and our course outline (markdown). Output a table of (domain, task, enabler, status, evidence_link) where status ∈ {covered, partial, missing}. Cite specific course slides.

antipattern.description: The README is a single-page summary; it omits the actual ECO (Examination Content Outline) tasks/enablers per domain. Agents that quote percentages without ECO tasks produce shallow study plans.
antipattern.reason: Anchor to PMI Standard for Earned Value (2024), PMBOK 7e, Agile Practice Guide, and the published 2026 ECO PDF.

antipattern.description: "Sustainability" and "Tailoring" are PMI buzzwords; LLMs hallucinate plausible-sounding but non-PMI definitions.
antipattern.reason: Anchor content to authoritative PMI sources: PMBOK 7e, Agile Practice Guide, Process Groups Practice Guide.

antipattern.description: Domain weights are not exam question counts; PMI uses scaled scoring per domain. Agents that promise "you must get 26% of Business Environment correct" are wrong.
antipattern.reason: PMI scaled scoring is not public. Use weights to allocate study effort, not to predict raw question distributions.

antipattern.description: Exam item bank evolves continuously after July 2026; static content goes stale within months of release.
antipattern.reason: Plan for ongoing revision. Treat curriculum as a living document, not a one-time PDF.

antipattern.description: Regional language exams (UA, RU, ES, ZH) lag English by 1–3 quarters; weights are the same but examples differ.
antipattern.reason: Translate examples with PMI vocabulary, not literal translation. Maintain language parity.

antipattern.description: Generating PMP mock questions with LLMs without human PMP-holder review before delivery.
antipattern.reason: LLMs invent plausible PMI vocabulary that does not appear in the ECO or source texts. Auto-generated PMP items distributed to candidates without review damage exam credibility and candidate preparation.

antipattern.description: Treating domain weights as direct question-count percentages.
antipattern.reason: PMI uses scaled scoring per domain; "26% Business Environment" does not mean 26% of raw questions map to that domain directly. Agents that promise specific question counts mislead candidates.

antipattern.description: Applying the 2026 ECO to candidates sitting before July 1, 2026.
antipattern.reason: Pre-cutover candidates are tested on the 2021 outline; using the new weights for them introduces wrong material and creates confusion about what to prioritize.

list.item: pdftotext (poppler) — Extract ECO PDF for ingestion into curriculum graph
pandoc — Convert course materials between markdown / docx / pptx for diffing
jq / yq — Manipulate the ECO topic graph as JSON/YAML
anki-cli / genanki (Python) — Generate flashcard decks from enabler list
pytest — Run a test that validates every mock question has a source citation
git + branch-per-cohort — Track curriculum versions per exam window
PMI.org (CCRS, exam scheduling) — Limited API; no public mock-exam API
Pearson VUE — Exam delivery vendor; no agent integration
PrepCast / PM Edu — Closed content; reference only
Anki (AnkiWeb / AnkiConnect) — Local API for deck push from mock-exam-author
Notion / Confluence — Host the ECO graph + study notes
GitHub / GitLab — Curriculum repo, PR-based review of generated questions
Quizlet — Sets API deprecated; avoid for new builds
Kahoot / Mentimeter — Cohort live-quiz delivery during instructor-led sessions
Anchor every generated question to a primary PMI source (PMBOK 7e, Agile Practice Guide, PfMP/PgMP standards) with page citation; reject questions without citations.
Rebuild the ECO graph from the official 2026 PDF on each PMI revision announcement; never edit it by hand.
Track candidate weak areas at the enabler level, not the domain level — domain-level coaching is too coarse.
Human-review 100% of auto-generated mock items before they reach candidates; PMP brand exposure to bad items is a credibility risk.
Maintain language parity for at least UA + EN if your audience is bilingual; translate stems with PMI vocabulary, not literal translation.
Pair certification prep with a real-project simulation (kanban board, risk register, EVM exercise) so candidates do not become test-taker-only PMs.
Sunset old (2021 outline) materials with a clear date stamp; do not silently overwrite — auditors and re-takers need both versions.

antipattern.description: LLMs invent PMI vocabulary that "sounds right" (e.g., "Strategic Tailoring Index").
antipattern.reason: Force a glossary check against PMBOK 7e index before accepting any term.

antipattern.description: Domain weight drift: agents fed old outlines (42/50/8) will silently regress.
antipattern.reason: Pin the 33/41/26 weights in the system prompt, not a retrieval doc that can be evicted.

antipattern.description: "Business Environment" is the largest growth area and most under-represented in legacy material — quotas alone do not fix shallow content; require new examples per enabler, not paraphrases.
antipattern.reason: Invest in original content creation, not repackaging.

antipattern.description: Distractor design is hard for LLMs — they often produce options that are obviously wrong or two-correct.
antipattern.reason: Run a self-critique pass on every generated question.

antipattern.description: Region/language: same weights, different idioms. Translation agents that copy English idioms into UA/PT lose realism.
antipattern.reason: Employ bilingual PMI-certified reviewers for regional variants.

antipattern.description: Citation hallucination: LLMs love fake page numbers. Validate citations by retrieving the cited PDF page text and checking for keyword overlap before publishing.
antipattern.reason: Build a validation hook into the publishing pipeline.

antipattern.description: Never let an agent decide if a candidate is "ready"; emit readiness signals (sub-domain confidence, mock score trend) and let a human instructor make the call.
antipattern.reason: Preserve human judgment on certification decisions.

antipattern.description: PDU/CCRS reporting: do not auto-submit PDUs on a candidate's behalf — PMI compliance issue; agents only draft.
antipattern.reason: Maintain audit trail and compliance responsibility with human instructors.

template.purpose: Weighted exam-style session scorer using 2026 ECO domain weights (33/41/26).
